note: Vectors computed directly from the shipped kernel. non-major-clean has no qualifying criteria and no reporting clock; major-incident-with-clock exercises the MAJOR branch with a full initial/intermediate/final reporting clock; major-incident-no-timestamp exercises the MAJOR branch when classification_at is missing (reporting_clock stays null, empty-input-adjacent finite path).

vectors.name: non-major-clean
vectors.description: All severity dimensions below threshold -- NON_MAJOR verdict, no reporting clock.

criteria_detail.article: DORA Art. 18(1)(d); RTS (EU) 2024/1772 data-losses criterion (confidentiality/integrity/availability impact)

output_payload.table_source: DORA (EU) 2022/2554 Art. 18 (classification criteria) + Art. 19 (reporting obligations/timelines); Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/1772 (RTS on classification criteria for major ICT-related incidents and significant cyber threats); Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/302 (ITS on standard forms/templates and timelines for major-incident reports). Numeric thresholds pinned at MODERATE confidence -- verify against final consolidated RTS/ITS text at legal review before relying on this verdict for an actual filing.
output_payload.note: Deterministic DORA (Art. 18) major-incident classification over caller-declared severity dimensions, plus a DORA Art. 19 reporting-clock (initial/intermediate/final) once classified major. See art-428-cyber-incident-clock.kernel.mjs for the analogous US notification-clock pattern. This kernel classifies and computes deadlines only; it does not itself transmit, file, or submit any regulatory notification, and it is not legal advice. Criticality of the affected function/provider (critical_services_affected) is a caller-supplied input, not computed or judged here.

vectors.name: major-incident-with-clock
vectors.description: Clients-affected + duration + geographic-spread + data-losses + economic-impact + critical-services + reputational all breach threshold -- MAJOR verdict with a full 4h/72h/1-month reporting clock.

criteria_detail.article: DORA Art. 18(1)(d); RTS (EU) 2024/1772 data-losses criterion (confidentiality/integrity/availability impact)

reporting_clock.note: Initial notification 4h, intermediate report 72h, final report 1 calendar month -- all clocked from classification_at (see kernel header for citation + confidence caveat).

output_payload.table_source: DORA (EU) 2022/2554 Art. 18 (classification criteria) + Art. 19 (reporting obligations/timelines); Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/1772 (RTS on classification criteria for major ICT-related incidents and significant cyber threats); Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/302 (ITS on standard forms/templates and timelines for major-incident reports). Numeric thresholds pinned at MODERATE confidence -- verify against final consolidated RTS/ITS text at legal review before relying on this verdict for an actual filing.

vectors.name: major-incident-no-timestamp
vectors.description: MAJOR verdict (data_losses independent trigger) but classification_at omitted -- reporting_clock stays null and a compliance_flag records the missing timestamp.

criteria_detail.article: DORA Art. 18(1)(d); RTS (EU) 2024/1772 data-losses criterion (confidentiality/integrity/availability impact)
